title: The Collapse of Time, Self, and Consciousness: Toward an Ontology of Awareness
abstract: Examining consciousness through physics and philosophy, the paper argues that time, self, and consciousness are memory-dependent constructs, leaving awareness as the only fundamental reality.

title: The Calibration Problem for Moral Intuition: Neurochemical Challenges to Intuitionist Moral Realism
abstract: Examining the calibration problem for moral intuition through the lens of neurochemical processes and their implications for intuitionist moral realism.
